summary: A structured path through the SQL Competence framework, with recommended activities at each stage.

learningStages.title: Stage 1: SQL Fundamentals
learningStages.summary: Build a solid foundation with single-table queries, basic filtering, and sorting.
suggestedActivities: Complete an interactive SQL tutorial (e.g., SQLBolt, W3Schools SQL).
Practice 20+ basic queries against a sample database.
Learn to read SQL error messages and fix syntax errors independently.

learningStages.title: Stage 2: Data Analysis with SQL
learningStages.summary: Advance to multi-table queries, aggregations, and analytical patterns.
suggestedActivities: Work through JOIN exercises with 3+ table schemas.
Learn window functions through progressive examples.
Practice writing CTEs to organize complex analytical queries.

learningStages.title: Stage 3: Schema Design & Integrity
learningStages.summary: Learn to design schemas, enforce integrity, and manage database changes.
suggestedActivities: Design a schema from a feature specification and write DDL.
Set up a migration tool and write reversible migration scripts.
Practice reading execution plans for queries you write.

learningStages.title: Stage 4: Performance & Optimization
learningStages.summary: Develop deep skills in query optimization, index design, and concurrency.
suggestedActivities: Analyze slow query logs and propose optimization strategies.
Design composite indexes for real query workloads.
Study isolation levels with hands-on concurrency experiments.

learningStages.title: Stage 5: Data Architecture & Governance
learningStages.summary: Lead data architecture decisions and establish organizational standards.
suggestedActivities: Design a multi-service data architecture with clear ownership boundaries.
Author SQL standards and review guidelines for your team.
Evaluate distributed database options for a real workload.

futureExpansions.title: Dialect-Specific Tracks
futureExpansions.summary: Specialized learning paths for PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQL Server, and BigQuery.

futureExpansions.title: Data Engineering Integration
futureExpansions.summary: Connecting SQL competence to broader data engineering skills: ETL, data pipelines, and orchestration.
